channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tea Party movement finds a new champion in Trump
item.description: Tea Party stalwarts who have seen their movement fizzle are finding a new champion in President Trump and see his second administration as a culmination and validation of their efforts. Sen. Ron Johnson (R-Wis.), who gave his first political speech at a Tea Party rally in Oshkosh, Wis.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Stacey Abrams slammed after defending $2 billion in Biden-era EPA funds to buy green energy appliances
item.description: Former Democratic Georgia gubernatorial candidate Stacey Abrams is under fire from conservatives after defending a $2 billion initiative under the Biden EPA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scott Perry says no one 'legitimately' on Medicaid will lose coverage
item.description: “I think what we can promise is that no one that is legitimately on Medicaid will lose their coverage, and I think that's important,” Perry said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: D.C.'s Black Lives Matter mural will be erased. Look back at the iconic street painting
item.description: Over the past five years, Washington, D.C.'s iconic Black Lives Matter street painting has served as a powerful symbol of activism and a gathering place for joy and resistance.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Canadians feel ‘under economic attack,’ frustration with US over Trump tariffs, annexation talk: ambassador
item.description: Canadian Ambassador to the U.S. Kirsten Hillman said citizens "don't really appreciate" President Donald Trump's talk about annexing Canada and feel "frustrated" over his tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump downplays business concerns about uncertainty from his tariffs and prospect of higher prices
item.description: President Donald Trump is dismissing business concerns over the uncertainty caused by his planned tariffs on a range of American trading partners and the prospect of higher prices.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Weeks after Epstein file fallout, a new deadline looms in the release of the RFK and MLK files
item.description: The deadline to submit a release plan for the declassification of the RFK and MLK files comes just a few weeks after the DOJ's fallout over its release of Epstein-related files.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its employees
item.description: Most of the 80,000 federal workers responsible for researching diseases, inspecting food and administering Medicare and Medicaid under the auspices of the Health and Human Services Department were emailed an offer to leave their job for as much as a $25,000 payment.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk and DOGE try to slash government by cutting out those who answer to voters
item.description: DENVER (AP) — For decades, conservatives in Congress have talked about the need to cut government deeply, but they have always pulled back from mandating specific reductions, fearful of voter backlash.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's transactional approach to diplomacy is a driving force on the world stage
item.description: Donald Trump's style of diplomacy is a whole lot like a high-stakes business deal, and his No. 1 goal is to come out of the transaction on top.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vance ramps up his clout in Trump world
item.description: Vice President Vance’s clout is on the rise, taking on the role of pot stirrer at the White House and on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-district Democrats face risky vote on GOP spending bill
item.description: House Democrats in districts carried by President Trump are confronting a potentially risky vote next week when GOP leaders are expected to put their partisan government funding bill on the floor.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Facing competition from Big Tech, states dangle incentives and loosen laws to attract power plants
item.description: Facing spiking energy demand, U.S. states are pressing for ways to build new power plants faster as policymakers try to protect residents from higher electric bills and keep their economies on track.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China learned from Trump's first trade war and changed its tactics when tariffs came again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The leaders of both Canada and Mexico got on the phone with President Donald Trump this past week to seek solutions after he slapped tariffs on their countries, but China’s president appears unlikely to make a similar call soon.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance says he was confronted by pro-Ukrainian protesters while walking with toddler daughter
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Saturday he was accosted by pro-Ukrainian protesters who scared his 3-year-old daughter a week after his tense exchange with the Ukrainian president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fetterman: 'Columbia pays for its failure' with $400M in grants axed
item.description: The campus was a hot spot for pro-Palestine protests after the Hamas attack on Oct. 7, 2023, sparked discourse in the region.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: FEMA cancels classes at national fire training academy amid federal funding cuts
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The country's preeminent federal fire training academy canceled classes, effective immediately, on Saturday amid the ongoing flurry of funding freezes and staffing cuts by President Donald Trump's administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans unveil bill to avoid shutdown and they're daring Democrats to oppose it
item.description: House Republicans have unveiled a 99-page spending bill that would keep federal agencies funded through Sept. 30. It would provide a slight boost to defense programs while trimming nondefense programs below 2024 budget year levels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk 's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP campaign chairman says voters will 'reward us' for the Trump-Musk DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Almost two months into President Donald Trump's second term, the chairman of the House Republican campaign committee is already predicting his party will pick up seats in the midterm elections some 20 months away. Rep. Richard Hudson, R-N.C.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is forcing a generational shift in GOP foreign policy. Here's how Republicans are responding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans in Congress have long been intent on countering America's rivals and spreading U.S. influence abroad.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House unleashes on 'rogue bureaucrats' after agency head refuses DOGE entry to headquarters
item.description: The president of the U.S. African Development Foundation filed a lawsuit against the Department of Government Efficiency after the cost-cutting team entered the agency's office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what happened during President Donald Trump's 7th week in office
item.description: Trump launched the week telling Americans that his administration would work to take "America’s destiny into our own hands" and vowed that "this will be our greatest era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New EPA guidance says spending items greater than $50,000 must get approval from DOGE
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency has issued new guidance directing that spending items greater than $50,000 now require approval from Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Homeland Security ends TSA collective bargaining agreement, in effort to dismantle union protections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Homeland Security said Friday that it is ending the collective bargaining agreement with the tens of thousands of frontline employees at the Transportation Security Administration, marking a major effort to dismantle union protections under the Trump administratio
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Diego Congress member pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: US tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: DOGE cuts all GSA employees across Nevada, senator says
item.description: President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) fired all General Services Administration employees across Nevada on Monday, according to Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin audit of Trump win finds not a single voting machine error
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — An audit of the November election won by President Donald Trump in swing-state Wisconsin found that not a single vote was counted incorrectly, altered or missed by tabulating machines.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, Fox 5
